text: Jarvis, what are the calendar items for Friday?
title: Morning Greeting

text: Morning Mike! Today is Friday, February 21. You have a 1:1 with Anika at 2 PM, and Ethan has piano with Mrs. Patterson.
title: Jarvis Morning Briefing

text: Just wanted to mention that working on the roadmap slides for Q4. We are hitting major bottleneck issues with /sms/send under load. This could be a blocker for the Vertex Health launch.
title: Work Update

text: Sure thing! That sounds critical. We should implement a connection pool or check the indexing on the underlying tables for /sms/send. I'll save this reasoning in our engineering scratchpad.
title: Jarvis Work Response

text: Quick log: Reviewing our household budget today. They requested our W-2 and bank statements. Will talk this over with Sarah.
title: Finance Update

text: Understood. I've compiled the spreadsheet numbers. I have prepared a checklist of documents in your shared folder. I've saved the 529 details.
title: Jarvis Finance Response

text: Quick log: Writing up user stories for our upcoming sprint. I am negotiating my senior PM salary and signing the Vertex Health offer letter. Hopefully we can align on this during the stakeholder review.
title: Work Update

text: Sure thing! Good focus area. Negotiation is key. Ensure the relocation package covers the Chicago to Frisco move. I can add this optimization to the backlog.
title: Jarvis Work Response

text: Hey Jarvis, Went for a run around the neighborhood. The pediatrician says Lily is growing on track. Feeling good about my routine.
title: Health Update

text: Got it, Mike. I've logged your vitals. Excellent news. The pediatric report is saved in Lily's folder. I've saved the checkup notes.
title: Jarvis Health Response

text: Quick update: Sarah and I are discussing screen-time rules for the kids. Lily is trying to teach Cooper how to sit using training treats. Hope tomorrow is a bit easier.
title: Parenting Update

text: Got it, Mike. Good planning. That's adorable! Cooper is responding well to Lily's positive cues. Parenting is definitely a journey.
title: Jarvis Parenting Response

text: Jarvis, Ethan is working on a school assignment at the table. He wants to build a clay model of Saturn for his science project. We want to make sure he stays motivated.
title: Kids School Update

text: School projects are fun. We have clay and foam rings in the garage storage shelves that will be perfect. His curiosity is really impressive.
title: Jarvis Kids School Response

text: Spent hours calibrating the telescope mount. Curiosity and Perseverance RTG decay rates differ. My ADHD hyperfocus really kicked in.
title: Neurodivergent Update

text: Interesting. Space science and tech is a great focus. MMRTG heat management is complex. I've archived your propulsion notes. I've saved your custom configurations.
title: Jarvis Neurodivergent Response

text: Just wanted to mention that prepping for the stakeholder meeting later today. Greg Holloway wants to drop scheduling portal to save time, but Anika thinks it is essential. Greg is not going to like this, but we have to push back.
title: Work Update

text: Got it, Mike. Got it, Mike. I suggest a phased rollout. Shipping a basic version of scheduling portal now and iterating later is a good compromise. I can prep some talking points for your meeting with Anika.
title: Jarvis Work Response

text: Quick update: Calculating some savings estimates. The refi quote saves us about $250 a month. Financial planning is key for the kids.
title: Finance Update

text: Got it, Mike. Budget tracking is updated. That's $3,000 annually. It could cover the Colorado trip or 529 plans. I've saved the 529 details.
title: Jarvis Finance Response

text: Anika and I were mapping out the sprint goals. Saying goodbye to my old colleagues in Chicago on my last day at MedFuture Systems. I want to discuss this with you before the sync.
title: Work Update

text: That is a common bottleneck. It's important to keep in touch with old colleagues; they are a great professional network. Should we schedule a calendar slot to do a deep dive?
title: Jarvis Work Response

text: Quick update: Took Lily to her medical checkup. I ran 5 miles but my knee felt tight. Need to focus on recovery.
title: Health Update

text: Logged that. That's good medical feedback. Great run! Remember to stretch your hamstrings to help the knee. Keep stretching that knee!
title: Jarvis Health Response

text: Quick update: Lily had a tough time at bedtime tonight. Cooper had a small accident on the living room rug during crate training. We need to stay consistent with them.
title: Parenting Update

text: Interesting. Toddlers are full of energy. Puppy training takes patience. Try using positive reinforcement and regular outdoor breaks. I can set a Roblox timer if needed.
title: Jarvis Parenting Response

text: Jarvis, Sarah attended the parent-teacher conference today. He is asking lots of questions about exoplanet atmospheric compositions. We want to make sure he stays motivated.
title: Kids School Update

text: School projects are fun. That's a great interest. I can summarize the atmosphere of Venus in a kid-friendly way. His curiosity is really impressive.
title: Jarvis Kids School Response

text: Just wanted to mention that writing scripts to process image datasets. I quantized a local LLM to run in memory. Lost track of time, it's 2 AM now.
title: Neurodivergent Update

text: Understood. I checked the telemetry feeds. Quantizing 70B models is heavy coding. I've saved settings in your scratchpad. I can pull more research papers.
title: Jarvis Neurodivergent Response

text: Time to log today's thoughts, Jarvis. CareConnect meetings took up most of the day. Dealt with bedtime tantrums but made it through. Logged my running mileage and stretched my knee. Ready for sleep.
title: Evening Journal Entry

text: Journal summary recorded. Have a peaceful night.
title: Jarvis Journal Summary
